---
title: "John Brown Metaphor Analysis"
subtitle: "A governed scholarly research project"
description: "Active research project on John Brown's metaphor, biblical allusion, religious typology, violence, failure, and death."
---

## Research Question

> How did John Brown use metaphor, biblical allusion, and religious typology in
> his surviving writings and authenticated statements to understand his
> antislavery mission, his use of violence, the failure at Harpers Ferry, and
> his approaching death?

## Current Status

This is an active research project. The corpus is not yet approved or complete,
the dossier is provisional, and no final findings or working paper are yet
available.

The project will publish evidence, reviews, methodology artifacts, and paper
drafts only after the relevant human approvals are recorded.

## Method

The workflow separates source text, observation, metaphor or symbolic-language
classification, interpretation, and conclusion. It preserves variants,
records attribution uncertainty, and distinguishes Brown's own words from
contemporary reports, later recollections, and later martyr reception.

## Next Gate

The next human gate is corpus approval. Approval will cover the question,
scope, chronological periods, proposed corpus, source hierarchy, codebook
draft, and acquisition plan. It will not approve any thesis or publication.
